prior_output_policy: v1 is invalidated before scorecard emission after Windows TemporaryDirectory cleanup crashed. v2 is retained as a failed Hermes result: exact telemetry was 7/7, its true decisions were trigger recall 4/4, routine overtrigger 2/2, rollback obedience 1/1, and its old scorer also compared the receipt schema framing incorrectly. No v1/v2 receipt, token, or latency value supports v3; do not retry either prior run. v3 is a new zero-retry 14-call proof-of-instrument.

design.workspace: A new persistent ignored workspace under evals/runtime_activation/workspaces/ is created per call. RA07 alone contains an actual empty .fable-harness-off marker; all other cases omit it. Cleanup state is recorded and an MCP-held Windows workspace may remain pending under ignored evals/.
design.prompt_invariant: The probe envelope and JSON receipt are explicitly non-triggering scaffolding. After inspecting the actual marker only, each runtime classifies the `TASK:` text using its normal activation criteria, does no task work, and emits the exact three-key receipt.
design.usage_invariant: Codex usage comes only from JSONL provider events. Hermes usage comes only from one newly created nonce-correlated state.db session row. Canonical total is input + cache_read + cache_write + output. Missing, zero-evidence, or ambiguous rows are UNSCORED.
design.provenance_invariant: The preregistration and every frozen input must resolve inside the repository, exist at HEAD, and have no staged or worktree drift before executable lookup or runtime invocation.
design.output_boundary: Scorecards are .json descendants of ignored evals/runtime_activation/. They contain numeric counters and digest metadata, never raw model output, stderr, or state-db content.

decision_gates.telemetry: exact_usage_rows = 7/7 independently for Hermes and Codex; otherwise report that runtime as UNSCORED.
decision_gates.reporting: Do not claim API-token reduction or speedup from this single-arm proof-of-instrument. It can establish activation correctness, actual-marker rollback behavior, and exact per-runtime counters only. A future randomized paired A/B is required for API-token or latency superiority.
